---
title: "Relational data model"
description: "How master records are normalized for analysis, export, and the dashboard."
date: last-modified
---

The transformation layer in `data_model/` converts the pipeline's nested JSONL master records into a normalized DuckDB model and exports CSV tables for Datasette or Supabase.

## Core entities

- `products`: canonical product records.
- `product_membership`: source membership and match provenance.
- `product_identifiers` and `product_synonyms`: normalized external identifiers and names.
- `essential_medicines`, `market_authorization`, `product_qualification`, and `igh_membership`: source-facing relational marts.
- `clinical_trials`, `clinical_trial_product_links`, `clinical_trial_terms`, `clinical_trial_results`, and `clinical_trial_raw`: consolidated clinical evidence.

## Implementation sources

- `data_model/schema/*.yaml` defines table contracts.
- `data_model/build_data_model.py` creates the DuckDB model and CSV exports.
- `data_model/views/create_views.sql` defines analytical and dashboard views.
- `data_model/generate_supabase_schema.py` generates PostgreSQL/Supabase DDL.

See [Master record schema](master-record-schema.qmd) for the upstream JSONL structure that feeds this layer.
